what_it_does: A static preview card (@dsCard group="Brand") showing the ConceptV wordmark in two contexts: a light/canvas pane displaying the real black wordmark PNG (primary), and a dark pane that switches to the yellow V mark — demonstrating the on-dark substitution rule.
observations: Two side-by-side panes: light (--bg-canvas) shows conceptv-wordmark-black.png; dark (--bg-dark) shows conceptv-v-yellow.png at 96px.
Corner tags label each pane: 'On canvas · primary' and 'On dark · use V mark'.
A code comment notes the dark pane swaps the wordmark for the V mark rather than inverting.
Body background uses --bg-canvas; panes use token backgrounds and a faint border divider.
standouts: The dark-context rule is enforced by substitution (full wordmark on light, V mark on dark) rather than recoloring the same asset.
conventions: References real logo assets under ../assets/logos/ (no fiction).
Tokens via var(--…) for backgrounds, borders, tag colors.
concerns: The dark pane V mark uses inline style overrides (filter:none; width:auto; height:96px) rather than a class — a small local hardcoded-px touch.
notes: The wordmark card — primary black wordmark on canvas, yellow V mark on dark, demonstrating the context-based mark substitution rule.
questions: Do conceptv-wordmark-black.png and conceptv-v-yellow.png exist at ../assets/logos/ as referenced?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — subtitle (real wordmark file; on dark, switch to the V mark) is exactly what the two panes show.
intention_signals: Specifies how the brand wordmark must be deployed across light and dark surfaces — full wordmark on canvas, V mark on dark — to keep the identity legible and correct per context.
novelty: The explicit rule to swap to the V mark (not invert the wordmark) on dark backgrounds.
contribution: Contributes the wordmark-usage rule (primary wordmark on canvas vs V mark on dark) and the real logo asset references that a fused system would keep as its logo-deployment guidance.
summary_for_embedding: A ConceptV brand preview card showing wordmark usage — the black wordmark on canvas (primary) and the yellow V mark on dark — demonstrating context-based mark substitution.
intention_for_embedding: To document the correct deployment of the brand wordmark across light and dark surfaces.
